title: Stenandrium thomense
taxonomy: Stenandrium thomense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Stenandrium and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is characterized by its unique appearance and growth habits. More specifically, Stenandrium thomense is classified as a member of the tribe Justicieae within the family Acanthaceae. Further classification details may be found in botanical resources or scientific databases.

intro: Stenandrium thomense, also known as the "Tomé's Stenandrium," is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Stenandrium thomense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and it may also be found in some traditional remedies and supplements.
